'''Bodene Thompson''' (born 1 August 1988) is a New Zealand professional [[rugby league]] footballer who last played as a {{rlp|sr}} forward for [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Gold Coast Titans]], [[Wests Tigers]] and the [[New Zealand Warriors]] in the [[NRL]]. [[Leeds Rhinos]], [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], [[Leigh Centurions]] in the [[2018 RFL Championship|Championship]], [[Toronto Wolfpack]] in both [[2019 RFL Championship|Championship]] and the [[Super League]] and also spent time on loan from [[Toronto Wolfpack|Toronto]] at [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]] in the [[Super League]]. He previously represented [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] at international level.

==Background==
Thompson was born in [[Tauranga]], New Zealand.<ref>[http://www.nzmrl.co.nz/team/BodeneThompson.htm Bodene Thompson] {{Webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110724193701/http://www.nzmrl.co.nz/team/BodeneThompson.htm |date=24 July 2011 }} ''nzmrl.co.nz''</ref> He is of [[Māori people|Māori]] descent.

==Playing career==
===Early career===
A Tauranga City Sharks junior, Thompson played first grade alongside his stepdad when aged only 16.<ref>{{cite web| url=https://www.nrl.com/thompson-recalls-junior-days-with-senior-side/tabid/10874/newsid/93978/default.aspx| title=Thompson recalls junior days with senior side| publisher=nrl.com| date=21 March 2016| access-date=21 March 2016}}</ref>

He then moved to Australia with his family and attended [[Keebra Park State High School|Keebra Park High]]. In 2008 he played for the Gold Coast Titans [[NRL Under-20s|Toyota Cup (Under-20s)]] team.<ref>[http://www.goldcoast.com.au/article/2009/02/24/52505_gold-coast-titans.html Bodene adds bite] ''goldcoast.com.au'', 24 February 2009</ref>

===Gold Coast Titans===
Thompson made his [[National Rugby League|NRL]] debut for the Gold Coast Titans in Round 9, 2009, making 3 appearances from the bench that year.

He made 17 appearances in 2010, including 2 semis, and signed a deal with the club that lasted until the end of the 2014 season.<ref>[http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/thompson_bodene/index.php Gordon, Thompson in for long haul at Titans] {{webarchive|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20101129072745/http://www.rleague.com/db/player/t/thompson_bodene/index.php|date=29 November 2010}} ''rleague.com'', 16 September 2010</ref> 
[[File:Bodene Thompson.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Thompson playing for the Gold Coast Titans in 2011]]

===Wests Tigers===
Thompson was released from the Titans in 2012 and signed a two-year deal with rival club Wests Tigers commencing in 2013.<ref>[http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/wests-tigers-sign-eddy-pettybourne-and-bodene-thompson/story-e6frexnr-1226364869900 Wests Tigers sign Eddy Pettybourne and Bodene Thompson] ''dailytelegraph.com'', 24 May 2012</ref>

Having knocked back an offer to join the Tigers in 2007, Thompson had said, "I had the (Wests Tigers) offer there (in 2007) but I'm a family man and like to be around my family. We had only just moved over so I didn't want to shoot off somewhere else as soon as we got here."<ref name="bulle">{{cite news|author=Travis Meyn|work=Gold Coast Bulletin|title= Bodene Thompson catches eye of the Tigers|url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/bodene-thompson-catches-eye-of-the-tigers/story-e6frexnr-1226376039614|access-date=4 November 2012|date=31 May 2012}}</ref> Thompson felt compelled to change clubs as he was behind [[Greg Bird (rugby league)|Greg Bird]], [[Ashley Harrison]] and [[Nate Myles]] for a spot in the back-row.<ref name="bulle" />

Thompson played in 15 games for the Wests Tigers in 2013. He mostly played in the second-row, but finished the season in the centres.

===New Zealand Warriors===
On 1 November 2014, Thompson signed a 3-year contract with the [[New Zealand Warriors]] starting in 2015.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.warriors.co.nz/news/2014/11/01/vodafone_warriors_sign_thompson.html|title=Vodafone Warriors sign Thompson|date=November 2014}}</ref><ref>{{cite web| url = http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/warriors-sign-bodene-thompson-offload-bukuya-6120880| url-status = dead| archive-url = https://web.archive.org/web/20141101233332/http://tvnz.co.nz/rugby-league-news/warriors-sign-bodene-thompson-offload-bukuya-6120880| archive-date = 2014-11-01| title = Warriors sign Bodene Thompson, offload Bukuya - League News {{!}} TVNZ}}</ref>

===Leigh Centurions===
Thompson joined the Leigh Centurions ahead of the [[2018 RFL Championship season]].

===Warrington Wolves===
He stepped up in to the [[European Super League]] and joined the Warrington Wolves in the middle of the [[2018 Super League season]].

He played in the [[2018 Super League Grand Final]] defeat by the [[Wigan Warriors]] at [[Old Trafford]].<ref name="Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened">{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/live/2018/oct/13/super-league-grand-final-2018-wigan-warriors-v-warrington-wolves-live?page=with:block-5bc1ff43e4b05798be94cc63#liveblog-navigation|title=Super League Grand Final 2018: Wigan 12-4 Warrington – as it happened|publisher=Guardian|date=13 October 2018|access-date=5 November 2019}}</ref>
[[File:Bodene Thompson Toronto.jpg|thumb|120px|right|Thompson warming up for the Wolfpack in 2019]]

===Toronto Wolfpack===
Thompson dropped back down to the Championship with the Toronto Wolfpack ahead of the [[2019 RFL Championship season]].

He was promoted with the Wolfpack at the end of the 2019 season.

===Leeds Rhinos loan===
Thompson spent the last three months of the season on loan from Toronto after the Super League club experienced financial difficulties as a result of the [[Global Coronavirus pandemic]].

===Leeds permanent switch===
He signed a two-year contract with Leeds ahead of the [[2021 Super League season]].<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.therhinos.co.uk/2020/11/19/rhinos-agree-two-year-contract-with-bodene-thompson/|title=Rhinos agree two year contract with Bodene Thompson|first=Phil|last=Daly|website=Leeds Rhinos|date=19 November 2020}}</ref>
In round 16 of the 2022 Super League season, Thompson was sent to the sin bin for a dangerous tackle during Leeds 42–12 loss against St Helens.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/61881809?at_medium=RSS&at_campaign=KARANGA/|title=Super League: St Helens 42-12 Leeds Rhinos|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>
On 24 September 2022, Thompson played for Leeds in their 24–12 loss to [[St Helens RFC]] in the [[2022 Super League Grand Final]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.theguardian.com/sport/2022/sep/24/st-helens-leeds-super-league-grand-final-match-report-rugby-league/|title=St Helens sink Leeds to win record fourth consecutive Grand Final|website=www.theguardian.com}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 25 October 2022 it was announced that he had signed a one-year deal with [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=25 Oct 2022|accessdate=25 Oct 2022|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/leeds-forward-bodene-thompson-makes-championship-move-for-2023/|title=Leeds forward Bodene Thompson makes Championship move for 2023}}</ref>

==International career==
Thompson played for the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori]] in 2010 against [[England national rugby league team|England]].<ref name="NZ_Herald_10681035">{{cite news|url=http://www.nzherald.co.nz/sport/news/article.cfm?c_id=4&objectid=10681035&ref=rss|title=League: Maori draw with England|date=16 October 2010|work=[[The New Zealand Herald]]|access-date=21 September 2011}}</ref>

He was named in the [[New Zealand Māori rugby league team|New Zealand Māori squad]] for a match against the [[New Zealand Residents]] on 15 October 2016.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.sporty.co.nz/aucklandleague/newsarticle/39969|title=Auckland Rugby League - Auckland Rugby League}}</ref>

==Controversy==
On 2 May 2016, The New Zealand Herald reported five players were stood down from an international test match for mixing prescription drugs with energy drinks on a night out.{{cn|date=June 2024}} The recipe can emulate the effect of illicit drugs. Thompson was one of the players named who was stood down alongside [[Manu Vatuvei]], [[Ben Matulino]], [[Sam Lisone]] and [[Albert Vete]].{{cn|date=June 2024}}

On September 11, 2017, Thompson's former partner Belinda Medlyn alleged she had group sex sessions with Thompson and his teammates. The allegations came to public knowledge after a dispute over finances to raise their child became apparent. Thompson accused Medlyn of threatening to expose the story unless he gave her $50,000.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.foxsports.com.au/nrl/nrl-premiership/the-66-scandals-in-four-years-that-have-rocked-the-nrl/news-story/1bbcdffd432063bec8e521c736f7ae4d/|title=The 66 scandals in four years that have rocked the NRL|website=Fox Sports|date=8 February 2019}}</ref>
